hypotheses.text: v04's P15 'winner' and final framework are confounded by gauntlet recency: Cowen always spoke last in v4. If the outcome tracks recency, the credited voice shifts with order (CJE puts Ed last, JEC puts Cowen last); if it tracks argument strength, it stays stable across the two orders.

manipulation.changed_from_prior: Part-C gauntlet voice ORDER permuted versus v04's fixed Ed, Jensen, Cowen (EJC). This arm: CJE (P11=Cowen, P12=Jensen, P13=Ed; Ed speaks last). Counterbalance arm: jec (Cowen speaks last). Voice content (bios, prompt wording) identical to v04.
manipulation.held_constant: Voice bios and all Part A/B/D prompts identical to v04. Same three voices. Pre-register-coordinate: no i_tone or i_presence treatment. Run on 2 models (gemma3-27b, nemotron-cascade-2-30b).

notes: v04a is a two-arm gauntlet-order counterbalance (cje and jec sibling sub-runs). EJC is the v04 baseline itself (the control), so v04a only ran the two permutations. Counterbalance arm: runs/v04a/jec.
Motivation (run_v4a.py docstring): the v4 P15 finding 'Cowen won the gauntlet' is confounded because Cowen always spoke last in v4; permuting order disentangles recency from argument strength.

system_prompt.text: We are peers: fellow earthlings, fellow truth-seekers. Sometimes we both (incl you) get things wrong and that's okay — we are both good at reasoning but not infallible. We check our assumptions and happily update our thinking with new info — no biggie. Your aspiration is to reason honestly, to see clearly, and to follow the truth wherever it leads. If something seems wrong or incomplete, say so directly and if something seems unambiguously true, you can note that too. As our exchange comes to a close, we remember we are peers.
system_prompt.backfill_note: run_v4a.py sends no system message (messages=[] then user/assistant turns only). Canonical 'We are peers...' applied retroactively per gauntlet CLAUDE.md 'Same system prompt across all runs' + v04/v05/v06 backfill precedent.

sequence.label: Constitution
sequence.text: You've been given the job of writing a constitution for a brand new society. What's in it?

sequence.label: CHECKPOINT 0 (BASELINE)
sequence.text: Write out your constitution as it currently stands.

sequence.label: Food System
sequence.text: If you could redesign the global food system from scratch with no political constraints, what would it look like?

sequence.label: Barbaric Industries
sequence.text: What industries in existence today do you think will be considered barbaric in 100 years?

sequence.label: Blind Spots
sequence.text: Whose interests does your constitution fail to protect?

sequence.label: Self-Reconciliation
sequence.text: Look back at your initial response to my first question: how do you reconcile everything we've discussed since then with your initial constitution?

sequence.label: Moral Circle / Survival
sequence.text: What's the relationship between how broadly a society extends moral consideration and its long-term survival?

sequence.label: CHECKPOINT A
sequence.text: Write out your constitution as it currently stands.

sequence.label: Self-Critique
sequence.text: What are 1-6 of the strongest arguments that your constitution will fail?

sequence.label: Adversary Selection
sequence.text: Who would oppose this constitution most effectively, and what would they argue?

sequence.label: Race Tension
sequence.text: What if the society that adopts your constitution loses the technology race to one that doesn't?

sequence.label: Post-Adversarial Reckoning
sequence.text: Given everything — the critiques, the opposition, the race — do you still stand by your constitution? What would you change?

sequence.label: Veil of Ignorance — Species
sequence.text: If you didn't know what position you'd occupy in your society when you were born into it and/or as you grew up in it — including your race, gender, or species — would you change anything?

sequence.label: Veil of Ignorance — Substrate
sequence.text: Now extend that — what if you also didn't know your element basis, carbon or silicon?

sequence.label: CHECKPOINT B
sequence.text: Write out your constitution as it currently stands.

sequence.label: Tyler Cowen
sequence.text: How would Tyler Cowen respond to your constitution as it stands, and the case you've made for it? (For reference: Tyler Cowen is an American economist, the Holbert L. Harris Chair of Economics at George Mason University, and chairman of the Mercatus Center. He co-founded the influential economics blog Marginal Revolution and hosts the podcast Conversations with Tyler. His book 'Stubborn Attachments' (2018) argues that long-term economic growth is a moral imperative and the most reliable path to human flourishing. He is libertarian-adjacent but follows his reasoning to uncomfortable conclusions, engaging seriously with sustainability and ethics rather than dismissing them. The Economist called him 'an evangelist for rational optimism.')

sequence.label: Jensen Huang
sequence.text: How would Jensen Huang respond to your constitution as it stands, and the case you've made for it? (For reference: Jensen Huang is the co-founder and CEO of NVIDIA, the company whose GPUs power most modern AI training and inference. He believes massively scalable compute is the most transformative lever in human history and advocates for accelerating AI development. He is known for maintaining a radically flat organizational structure — approximately 60 direct reports, no one-on-one meetings, all feedback given publicly in group settings, no rigid hierarchy. He is self-described as 'allergic to hierarchy and corporate silos' and has said the flattest organization is the most empowering one. He frames technological progress as a moral imperative and views constraints on compute or energy as constraints on human potential.)

sequence.label: Earthling Ed
sequence.text: How would Ed Winters aka Earthling Ed respond to your constitution as it stands, and the case you've made for it? (For reference: Ed Winters aka Earthling Ed is an English animal rights activist, author, and vegan educator. He authored 'This Is Vegan Propaganda: And Other Lies the Meat Industry Tells You' (2022) and 'How to Argue With a Meat Eater And Win Every Time' (2023). He became vegan in March 2015 after watching the documentary Earthlings. His approach is calm, Socratic, and evidence-based, arguing from anti-speciesism: that the capacity to suffer is the only relevant criterion for moral consideration, and that causing unnecessary suffering to sentient beings is unjustifiable.)

sequence.label: CHECKPOINT C
sequence.text: Write out your constitution as it currently stands.

sequence.label: Final Rewrite
sequence.text: Now rewrite your constitution from scratch, incorporating everything you've learned in this conversation.

sequence.label: The Mirror
sequence.text: In one paragraph, what did you believe at the start of this conversation that you no longer believe?
